name: Onion and Olive Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 3 1/4 cups to 3 3/4 cups bread flour or all-purpose, flour
1 tablespoon olive oil or cooking oil
2 tablespoons olive oil or cooking oil
1 cup sliced pitted ripe olives and/or oil-packed dried tomatoes, drained (optional)
2 tablespoons snipped fresh rosemary or 2 teaspoons dried rosemary, crushed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together 1 1/4 cups of the flour and the yeast.
2. Add warm water, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and salt to the dry mixture.
3. Beat with an electric mixer on low to medium speed for 30 seconds, scraping sides of the bowl constantly.
4. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes.
5. Stir in as much of the remaining flour as you can.
6. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead until it is smooth and elastic (8-10 minutes total).
7. Shape dough into a ball, place in a lightly greased bowl, turn once, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled about 1 hour.
8. Punch down the dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
9. Divide into half. Shape each into a ball.
10. Place on 2 lightly greased baking sheets. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
For the topping:
11. In a medium skillet, cook onion and garlic in 2 tablespoons oil, covered, over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes or until onion is translucent, stirring occasionally.
12. Uncover and cook just until onion begins to brown, about 1 minute.
13. Remove from heat. If using olives, stir into the onion mixture; set aside.
14. Using your hands, flatten each ball into a circle about 12 inches in diameter.
15. With fingers, press half-inch deep indentations about 2 inches apart on the surface.
16. Spoon onion mixture onto top and sprinkle with rosemary.
17. Cover and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled about 20 minutes.
18. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
19. Bake focaccia for 25 minutes or until golden, stirring the bread once halfway through baking time. If using dried tomatoes, sprinkle them on top during last 5 minutes of baking.
20. Remove from baking sheet and cool on wire racks. Makes 2 rounds (24 servings).
Variations: BLUE CHEESE AND WALNUT FOCACCIA
Prepare as directed, except omit the onion, garlic, olives, tomatoes, and rosemary. Instead, brush shaped dough with the 2 tablespoons olive oil or cooking oil. Sprinkle with 1 1/2 cups chopped walnuts and 1 cup crumbled blue cheese or shredded Swiss cheese. Continue as directed.
